boundary_records.boundary_statement: LPS dose, route, schedule, and timing must be preserved; pre-injury conditioning, repeated priming, post-injury treatment, and chronic reactivation are not interchangeable.
Resident microglia, recruited macrophages, monocytes, endothelial cells, and mixed homogenate cultures require separate cell-population labels.
Protective innate immune conditioning cannot be inferred from generic inflammation; pathway routing through TLR4/TRIF/IRF3/MyD88/CXCL10/IL-10/IL-1 must be tracked.
M1/M2 language is only a historical shorthand and should be replaced or qualified with marker/state, timing, and assay boundaries.
Spinal cord ischemia, traumatic SCI, TBI, and brain ischemia comparators can generate mechanisms but should not be pooled without injury-model labels.
Chronic SCI LPS/plasticity findings are valuable but should not be interpreted as acute preconditioning without chronicity labels.
ODC-SCI/database rows are candidate evidence until dataset/source extraction confirms intervention, timing, and endpoint details.
Low-dose LPS is a discovery platform for Aim 1 regulatory-node discovery and should not be framed as a clinical implant/reconstructive-surgery intervention.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Direct LPS preconditioning has enough first-pass SCI evidence to support a mechanistic discovery claim, centered on timed innate immune reprogramming rather than broad inflammatory suppression.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Direct LPS preconditioning has enough first-pass SCI evidence to support a mechanistic discovery claim, centered on timed innate immune reprogramming rather than broad inflammatory suppression.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Repeated systemic LPS can induce a spinal microglia-endothelial protective state in spinal ischemia contexts, with IL-1/IL-1R1 signaling acting as a key boundary condition.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Repeated systemic LPS can induce a spinal microglia-endothelial protective state in spinal ischemia contexts, with IL-1/IL-1R1 signaling acting as a key boundary condition.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: TLR pathway routing is a central determinant of whether innate stimulation is protective or damaging, so adaptor/cytokine boundaries must be retained in every synthesis row.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: TLR pathway routing is a central determinant of whether innate stimulation is protective or damaging, so adaptor/cytokine boundaries must be retained in every synthesis row.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Peripheral monocytes and endothelial cells are credible upstream regulatory compartments for host conditioning and should be carried into Aim 1C regulatory-node extraction.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Peripheral monocytes and endothelial cells are credible upstream regulatory compartments for host conditioning and should be carried into Aim 1C regulatory-node extraction.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: TLR4 and inflammatory signaling after SCI have repair-supporting as well as injury-driving roles, making complete anti-inflammatory framing too coarse for this project.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: TLR4 and inflammatory signaling after SCI have repair-supporting as well as injury-driving roles, making complete anti-inflammatory framing too coarse for this project.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Chronic or post-injury LPS can reveal latent spinal plasticity and microglial-state capacity, but it is consensus-ready only as a boundary/comparator to preconditioning.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Chronic or post-injury LPS can reveal latent spinal plasticity and microglial-state capacity, but it is consensus-ready only as a boundary/comparator to preconditioning.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: In vitro, ODC-SCI, and microbiome-host-conditioning rows provide candidate validation paths but require source extraction before they become high-confidence mechanistic evidence.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: In vitro, ODC-SCI, and microbiome-host-conditioning rows provide candidate validation paths but require source extraction before they become high-confidence mechanistic evidence.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: LPS should be treated as a discovery platform for endogenous protective programs; translational host conditioning before implants or reconstruction should route through safer analogs.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: LPS should be treated as a discovery platform for endogenous protective programs; translational host conditioning before implants or reconstruction should route through safer analogs.
